info.title: arbor
info.description: Render a directory structure similar to the Windows tree command

options.name: --files
options.description:
Include files in the tree output (similar to
`tree /f`)
1 includes direct children, etc

options.name: --depth
options.description:
Limit traversal depth. 0 shows only the root,
1 includes direct children, etc

options.name: --ascii
options.description:
Render connectors using ASCII characters
(similar to `tree /a`)
values
pattern (supports multiple)

options.name: --details-field
options.description:
Include specific metadata fields (size, time,
perm, owner, group). Supports comma-separated
values

options.name: --include
options.description:
Include only files matching the provided glob
pattern (supports multiple)

options.name: --exclude
options.description: Exclude files matching the provided glob

options.name: --ext
options.description:
Filter files by extension, e.g. `--ext .cs`
or `--ext json` (supports multiple)

options.name: --include-dir
options.description:
Include only directories matching the
provided glob pattern (supports multiple)

options.name: --exclude-dir
options.description:
Exclude directories matching the provided
glob pattern (supports multiple)

options.name: --export
options.description:
Choose the export format: text, json, or
markdown

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write the exported tree to a file instead of
stdout
